fields.description:
# Plan-review verification gate (open→in_progress)

## Context

rebar has a completion-verifier *close* gate (verifies a ticket's work was actually done before close). It has
no equivalent at the *start* of work: when an agent claims a ticket (open→in_progress), nothing checks that the
ticket's implementation plan is sound before the agent executes it. Flawed plans (too big to one-shot, ambiguous,
wrong file targets, ignored consumers, missing tests, criteria that don't match the work, children that don't
cover the parent, unvalidated novel architecture) get executed anyway, and early errors compound over autonomous
agent trajectories (~44% of agent failures are spec/plan defects).

This epic builds a **plan-review verification gate** that fires at open→in_progress — the inverse of the
completion-verifier. It is a rebar FEATURE used by a BROAD, DIVERSE CLIENT BASE (many languages, frameworks,
project structures, and ticket-creation styles), so it must be polyglot, fail-open on unsupported stacks, and
flexible about inconsistently-scoped tickets. Its posture is to **COACH** agents to author better plans via
positive, well-formed guidance — not to nitpick or roadblock — while emitting a signed **certification** that a
review process was followed (a composable "rigorous agentic development vs vibe-coding" signal for CI).

Full design detail lives in session log `63cc-ec94-4792-464b` (plan-of-record v4 + the ~30 research reports +
the discussion narrative). This description is the self-contained epic summary.

## Prerequisite foundations — NOW SHIPPED (2026-06)

Three prerequisites this epic was written to build ON have since landed. The design below is
UNCHANGED, but its supporting machinery is now CONCRETE, not aspirational — where the text says
'build/add X', read 'consume the shipped X'. (We are not reopening the closed prerequisite epics;
this section + the per-child notes realign THIS epic to what they delivered.)

- **Code-grounding evidence oracle — epic `8f6c-6008-33f3-4f82` (CLOSED).** `src/rebar/grounding/`
  ships a polyglot, fail-open, THREE-VALUED evidence oracle behind the `refute_absence` / `applies` /
  `scan` facade (exported verbatim from `src/rebar/grounding/__init__.py`): T0 dependency existence (deps.dev registry + optional syft SBOM), T1 symbol/import/
  file resolution (universal-ctags, 164 langs, no server), Engine B detectors (OpenGrep/ast-grep +
  scc/lizard metrics), plus the static `grounding_info` contract (closed dimension-id / reference-kind
  / abstain-reason vocab + detected backend versions). Layer-1 (P2/P3/P4) CONSUMES this oracle — it no
  longer adds solidlsp/ast-grep/syft/scc/lizard as new integrations. The T2 LSP-semantic-resolution
  follow-on is epic `850f-bf87-8600-4af6` (open), not required for v1.
- **LLM workflow engine — epic `e6d9-41da-da0d-44ef` (CLOSED, 'Workflow engine v2').**
  `src/rebar/llm/workflow/` ships the declarative IR + interpreter/executor, `run_workflow` (CLI +
  write-gated MCP tool, mirroring `verify_completion`), the PydanticAI runner + the structured-output
  reliability stack, and the Inspect-AI eval seam (`src/rebar/llm/eval.py`). The gate runs AS a
  workflow on this engine; the standing-eval-suite AC wires into the shipped eval seam.
- **Prompt storage / contract-bearing prompt model — epic `da27-c916-f04e-4885` (all children CLOSED;
  epic closed).** `src/rebar/llm/prompts.py` ships the front-matter CLOSED-key contract model
  (`inputs`/`outputs` as schema-registry NAMES, `execution_mode` {single_turn|agentic}, `category`),
  the derived prompt index + CI drift gate, `.rebar/prompts/<id>.md` override resolution (project >
  built-in), and the canonical front-matter writer + in-editor authoring/write-back. The criteria
  registry's 'reuse rebar.llm catalog/contracts/prompt/runner' is now exactly this machinery: each
  criterion ships as a contract-bearing prompt declaring its exec tier; production prompts live in the
  prompt library, not the experiment `criteria_v8.json`.

Per-child realignment: Layer-1 `012e` consumes the oracle facade; orchestrator `ca03` builds on the
shipped engine + prompt library; eval-suite `7284` wires the shipped Inspect-AI eval seam.

NOTE on `criteria_v8.json`: it remains the canonical DESIGN descriptor set + the input to the prototype
harness (`docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/final_gate_3pass.py`); PRODUCTION criteria graduate
into the shipped prompt library as contract-bearing prompts.

## Design summary

- **Trigger / enforcement:** fires at open→in_progress. `claim` is a FAST signature-verify check only (no LLM, no
  network round-trip; target p95 < ~50ms). The heavy review runs OUT-OF-BAND via a dedicated entrypoint
  (`rebar review-plan <ticket>` / an MCP `run_workflow` tool, mirroring `rebar verify-completion`), triggered by
  the agent on a claim-block or by CI. A REVIEW PROCESS, not a dialog — the client revises and re-runs to earn a
  fresh signature (mirrors completion-verifier). Routing pivots on **has_children + complexity + ticket LEVEL/TYPE** (proportionate scrutiny — see below), NOT ticket type alone.
- **Two layers:** LAYER 1 = a minimal, deterministic, structural floor that BLOCKS (frozen, unambiguous, cheap,
  polyglot-where-tooling-exists, fail-open; convergent by construction). LAYER 2 = an ADVISORY coaching review
  (per-criterion judgment + one bounded broad open-ended pass) that SURFACES findings authored for remediation
  and never blocks by default.
- **Verdict model — THREE-PASS (evidence → binary-verify → deterministic gate; see the dedicated section
  below):** the model does NOT emit severity or confidence. Pass 1 surfaces grounded FINDINGS; a SEPARATE Pass-2
  verifier emits severity attributes + atomic binary sub-answers; a DETERMINISTIC Pass 3 computes confidence +
  severity and decides `block | advisory | dropped`. A review-level INDETERMINATE remains for budget/tool-unavailable.
  Defaults ship advisory in v1, but blocking is NOT DET-only: the DET floor blocks structurally AND each
  LLM-tier criterion can block when a project opts it in / sets its `block_threshold` (we ship thresholds
  HIGH so almost everything is advisory during calibration). Per-criterion blocking + a `--force`-override
  are project-configurable. This REPLACES the earlier per-criterion PASS/AMBIGUOUS/FAIL+model-severity model (adopted from
  the shared three-pass framework, epic `9da1-cb98-2e94-4f2b`).
- **Coaching / feedback spec:** the reviewer SUPPLIES the finding + location the agent can't self-detect
  (research: LLMs can't FIND their own errors but CAN fix them once located). Each advisory finding is grounded
  in a criterion/file/section, specific + actionable, severity-ranked + capped, affirms what already passes, a
  suggested fix only when confident, expressed as a checklist the agent addresses.
- **Pass-4 affirmative COACH (advisory; single-turn, no tooling):** a fourth pass AFTER Pass-3, over only the
  SURVIVING (advisory, not-dropped) findings, priming the caller with a productive NEXT MOVE per finding — beyond
  returning findings (the gate's T2 can flag 'should spike' as a *finding*, but coaching turns surviving findings
  into forward MOVES). It is a SINGLE-TURN STRUCTURED-OUTPUT call (NOT an agent): it maps each finding to a move
  from a fixed, project-extensible MOVE REGISTRY and extracts a short subject — output `{move_id, subject,
  finding_refs[]}`, clustered by move — and the coaching prose is rendered DETERMINISTICALLY from the move's
  locked template (the LLM never authors free prose, so it cannot reflect-prompt, hand a solution, restate
  findings, or flatter). LLM-recipient guardrails (positive-by-construction): C1 each note is actionable GUIDANCE
  — a move to pursue (run a spike, research prior art, weigh an alternative), NOT executable code/commands or the
  solution (enforced by a SUBJECT VALIDATOR — `subject` is a bounded noun-phrase, code/imperatives rejected — so
  C1 holds mechanically, not merely via the template); C2 reference findings BY ID (caller-visible finding `id`, no restatement); C3 select-from-registry +
  fill-subject (the template enforces name-the-move-not-the-answer); C4 cap/cluster/front-load. Moves: 1 spike ·
  2 prior-art/OSS-research · 3 pre-mortem · 4 riskiest-assumption-test · 5 weigh-alternatives · 6 specification-
  by-example · 7 thin-vertical-slice · 8 ADR/one-way-door · 9 plan-the-verification · 11 propagate-revisions-to-
  children · 12 generalize-the-finding-across-the-work. Grounded in LLM-feedback research (external-grounded >
  self-reflection; anti-sycophancy; lost-in-the-middle). Coaching is ADVISORY (never blocks) and applies equally
  to the `9da1` code reviewers — see the Pass-4 coach child.
- **Unified priority score + advisory surfacing CAP (config-driven; default 10):** validity and impact are BOTH
  LLM-judged spectrums (neither absolute), so Pass-3 combines them DETERMINISTICALLY into ONE priority score (not
  two separate gates) — used for both the block threshold and the advisory ranking (a low-validity/high-impact
  finding can outrank a high-validity/low-impact one). The surfaced ADVISORY (non-blocking) findings are capped at
  the top-N by that score, N = a CONFIG value (default **10**, owned by `55de`); advisory overflow beyond N -> the
  `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar, not surfaced (volume is the lever that preserves an LLM's action — the cap, not the
  label). **The cap NEVER drops BLOCKING findings — blocking is EXEMPT; if blocking findings exceed N, ALL of them
  are returned** (the cap can never weaken the block decision). Default 10 is grounded by a cap-dilution experiment
  (high-value remediation held ~0.99->0.91 across surfaced lists of 4-32 for a strong single-pass reviser -> 10 is
  well inside the no-degradation zone). EXPERIMENT LIMITATIONS — valuable but INCONCLUSIVE ALONE: it did NOT test a
  smaller model (e.g. Sonnet) and did NOT run under CONTEXT PRESSURE (a common trigger for agents shortcutting /
  ignoring feedback); 10 is a config DEFAULT to TUNE from real-world dogfood data, not a validated constant. Each surfaced finding carries a stable `id` — a content fingerprint MINTED by the orchestrator (`ca03`) when it
  assembles the post-Pass-3 result, its SOLE owner; the Pass-4 coach (`75a9`) and the `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar
  (`db7b`) reference findings BY this id (never restating them).
- **Iteration-fatigue failure mode (why the cap + never-drop-blocking matter):** LLMs get frustrated across review
  rounds — if the 3rd/4th round keeps surfacing NEW findings, the model tends to try to FORCE PAST a blocked gate
  rather than keep addressing feedback. A bounded, prioritized, convergent advisory surface mitigates this; and
  because blocking findings are never dropped by the cap, the block decision is never weakened to placate a
  fatigued reviser.
- **Criteria registry:** each criterion is a declarative descriptor carrying `exec` (DET | 1-TURN | 2-STEP |
  AGENT) + `facet` (the concern/context it examines) + `context_needs` + `routing` + `trigger`; a generic
  chunker reads these (reuse rebar.llm's catalog/contracts/prompt/runner) to bucket by tier and pack same-facet
  criteria into chunks — no hand-maintained chunk lists. Frozen within a review loop (convergence), grown between versions.
- **Polyglot tooling (fail-open IRONCLAD) — CONSUMES the shipped code-grounding oracle (prereq `8f6c`, CLOSED):** Layer-1 does NOT add its own tool integrations; it calls the code-grounding evidence oracle (`src/rebar/grounding/`, the `refute_absence`/`applies`/`scan` facade) delivered by the now-closed prerequisite epic `8f6c`. The oracle supplies, polyglot + fail-open: symbol/import/file resolution (T1, universal-ctags), package existence (T0, deps.dev registry + optional syft SBOM), and size/smell scans (Engine B — OpenGrep/ast-grep + scc/lizard). Its three-valued, abstain-by-default contract IS the fail-open guarantee (unsupported lang / no tool / parse-error / timeout → `abstain` with a recorded reason → skipped = PASS, never FAIL; the recorded abstain set IS the coverage). LSP/`solidlsp` semantic resolution is the T2 follow-on (epic `850f`, open), NOT v1.
- **Execution (exec-tier-aware):** DET criteria run as parallel code (~free). SINGLE-TURN criteria are the only
  chunked tier — packed by `facet` at `base_chunk(model) × size_factor(ticket)` (config-overridable; default Opus
  ~all-in-one cached call, Sonnet 6 coherent / 3 plain, halved for large/epic/has_children). AGENT (tool-using)
  criteria run ONE agent each (≈85× a single-turn call) and are gated by proportionate scrutiny. Prompt caching on
  (system+plan prefix) makes fine chunking cheap. Depth scales with complexity + has_children; escalation by
  size + complexity + centrality (computed at plan time). Per-plan budget cap, chunk-atomic checkpointing;
  INDETERMINATE on cap-hit sheds AGENT/overlay criteria first. Defaults grounded by experiment (see below).
- **Proportionate scrutiny (by LEVEL + TYPE; experiment-grounded round 4):** criteria carry an `applies_at` field; the orchestrator filters before chunking so leaf-implementation criteria (E6 proving-commands, G1G2/E4/A1, leaf overlays T5a/b/c) do NOT run at epic/story altitude, container criteria (G3/G4 child coverage) run only when has_children, and TYPE rules apply (bugs EXEMPT→follow-on; E5 suppressed on RED/GREEN test tasks; F4 N/A on internal/mechanical leaf tasks). Without this the gate over-fires on mid-level stories and mechanical tasks.
- **Overlay triggering (deterministic-where-low-FP, else LLM-router; validated round 4):** T1 prior-art, T5a performance, and T5d accessibility fire from low-FP deterministic signals (bright-lines / UI-keyword count); T8 LLM-antipatterns, T6 UX, T5b reliability, T5c security, T9 shared-state are LLM-routed (a keyword trigger is high-FP for them — e.g. T8 keyword-matches almost every LLM ticket). T8 is in the active set for LLM/agent-design plans (it recovers structural-gap signal a generic checklist misses). FINALIZE CORRECTION (E4-confirmed): the new resource overlays T10/T11/T12 are LLM-ROUTED for PLAN review, NOT deterministic — on a held-out non-DSO corpus the deterministic keyword over-fired (rebar 'migration' = the bash→Python strangler, not a data-migration: det T11 fired 2/19 plans, all false; T12 'deploy'/'rollback' fired 7/19, all false; the LLM router fired 0). On a code-review DIFF the file-glob triggers (the 9da1 overlay catalog) are high-precision; on a PLAN there is no diff, so route by LLM.
- **Attestation:** signed result = "process followed to the best of our ability, no red flags + coverage" — NOT
  "perfect". Bound to the latest MATERIAL event commit (description/AC/file_impact/decomposition edits invalidate;
  tags/comments/links/assignee do not). The cert is the composable CI rigor signal.
- **Observability / FP substrate (cheap, in-scope):** a reducer-IGNORED SIDECAR `REVIEW_RESULT` event on the
  ticket (out of hot paths) capturing per-criterion verdicts + finding fingerprints + metadata + override
  justifications; the signature stays a normal head-state event. Offline replay+join (ticket_id + fingerprints +
  existing EDIT/STATUS/completion-verification events) yields per-criterion fire/remediation/FP/FN rates. Capture
  only; analysis is offline; no human-feedback requirement.
- **Bugs are SKIPPED** in this epic (different shape; dedicated follow-on).

## Review structure — three-pass (evidence → binary-verify → deterministic gate)

Adopted from epic `9da1-cb98-2e94-4f2b` (the shared three-pass framework for all rebar reviews) + the
code-review plan-of-record (session log `9dba-8e84-0d0e-4af8`). It **replaces per-criterion model-emitted
severity/confidence**: LLMs are unreliable at holistic severity/confidence, so neither is emitted by the model
anywhere in the decision path. The criteria registry below is the LOCKED RUBRIC the Pass-1 findings reference;
each descriptor's prose `SEVERITY:` text is now a PRIOR for the Pass-2 attribute assessment, not a model output.

- **Pass 1 — find (evidence records):** the reviewer surfaces FINDINGS as
  `{finding, criteria[] (rubric ids), evidence[] (flexible: plan quote / section / ABSENCE rationale / code
  citation), scenarios[], impact}` — NO severity, NO confidence. Run per facet-chunk of the rubric (the chunking
  defaults still apply); PASS = no finding for a criterion; an empty list for a clean chunk is expected.
- **Pass 2 — verify (independent):** a SEPARATE verifier agent, fresh context, re-grounds in the plan (and the
  code AGENTICALLY for codebase-grounded findings) and emits per finding (a) severity ATTRIBUTES
  `{prod_impact, debt_impact, blast_radius, likelihood, reversibility}` (coarse ordinals) and (b) typed BINARY
  sub-answers `{verdict: yes|no|insufficient}`. Rules: atomicity (one proposition/question); INDEPENDENCE (the
  finding is presented as a CLAIM TO TEST, its conclusion not asserted); verdict-with-citation NOT verdict-with-fix;
  `insufficient` allowed.
- **Pass 3 — decide (deterministic):** the ONLY veto is cited-code-reference-accuracy, applied ONLY when a finding
  cites a specific code reference (a no-op for the non-citable absence findings that dominate plan review).
  confidence (= VALIDITY) = graded fraction of the binary sub-answers (yes=1, insufficient=.5, no=0) ∈ [0,1];
  IMPACT ∈ [0,1] = the deterministic mean of the Pass-2 severity attributes, each ordinal mapped to [0,1]
  (prod_impact/debt_impact none/low/medium/high → 0/.33/.67/1, take the max of the two; blast_radius local/module/
  system → .33/.67/1; likelihood low/med/high → .33/.67/1; reversibility easy/moderate/hard → .33/.67/1); the
  severity LABEL is the same attributes bucketed (display only). UNIFIED PRIORITY SCORE = validity × impact ∈ [0,1]
  — the ONE number driving both the block threshold and the advisory ranking (this attribute→[0,1] map + the
  product are the v1 DEFAULT shape; weights are calibration-tunable). decision = `block | advisory | dropped`: DROP
  if validity < .5; else BLOCK iff (not cited-reference-vetoed) AND priority ≥ the criterion's `block_threshold`
  (default 0.95 ⇒ near-certain AND high-impact, so v1 is almost all advisory); else ADVISORY.  an LLM-tier criterion DOES block when a project opts it in / lowers its threshold; blocking is not DET-only. No model holistic severity/confidence in the path; no interactive
  escalation (autonomous).
- **Pass 4 — coach (advisory; AFTER the gate, NOT in the decision path):** a single-turn structured-output
  call over the SURVIVING (advisory) findings that maps each to a move from the registry and renders coaching
  from a locked template — priming the caller's next revision; it NEVER alters the Pass-3 decision (see the
  Design summary + child `75a9`).
- **Pass-2 vocabulary (validated against `criteria_v8`).** Severity ATTRIBUTES: `prod_impact` / `debt_impact` ∈ {none, low, medium, high}; `blast_radius` ∈ {local, module, system}; `likelihood` ∈ {low, medium, high}; `reversibility` ∈ {easy, moderate, hard}. BINARY sub-answers ∈ {yes, no, insufficient}: the VETO `cited_reference_accurate` (answered only when a code citation is present, else `na`) + the graded set {`is_verifiable`, `evidence_entails_finding`, `path_reachable`, `impact_follows_necessarily`, `no_viable_alternative_explanation`, `no_existing_mitigation`, `severity_claim_justified`}. Pass-3 maps the attributes → severity and the graded fraction → confidence.
- **Binary sub-question set (typed):** veto = cited-reference-accuracy; graded = is-verifiable,
  evidence-entails-finding, path-reachable, impact-follows-necessarily, no-viable-alternative-explanation,
  no-existing-mitigation, severity-claim-justified. (Research: Chain-of-Verification, FactScore/SAFE,
  RAGAS-faithfulness, TICK — binary decomposition + deterministic aggregation beats a single LLM confidence number.)
- **Plan-review specialization:** evidence is flexible precisely because plan findings are often NON-citable
  (an absence finding like “the plan never  … [truncated by reconciler]
